articles.title: Klopp, small shinpads and whether he really is boring - Milner in his own words
articles.summary: James Milner - the Premier League's record appearance holder, speaks to reporter Kelly Somers about his career - including a debut goal for hometown club Leeds, a dramatic Champions League semi-final ...

articles.title: Maguire sent off as Bournemouth fight back to draw with Man Utd
articles.summary: Despite taking the lead twice, Manchester United are held to a 2-2 draw at Bournemouth and have Harry Maguire sent off as they drop points in the race for a top-four finish in the Premier League.

articles.title: Alisson out of Brazil squad and will miss Brighton
articles.summary: Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson will not face Brighton on Saturday because of injury and has withdrawn from the Brazil squad for their friendlies later this month.

articles.title: Man City's Trafford to start final but Arteta opts not to name Arsenal keeper
articles.summary: Manchester City boss Pep Guardiola confirms James Trafford will be in goal for Sunday's Carabao Cup final against Arsenal.

articles.title: 'It was chaos' - referee frustrates Carrick as Bournemouth hold Man Utd
articles.summary: Manchester United manager Michael Carrick was left frustrated by what he considered to be inconsistent refereeing decisions during his side's 2-2 draw at Bournemouth.

articles.title: 'There isn't much to discuss' - Iraola unfazed by penalty decisions
articles.summary: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola says "there isn't much to discuss" when asked about the controversial penalty decisions during his sides 2-2 with Manchester United, a draw which takes Bournemouth's ...

articles.title: Five-star Leipzig thrash Hoffenheim to boost Champions League hopes
articles.summary: RB Leipzig thrash Hoffenheim 5-0 in the Bundesliga to secure a vital win in the race to finish in the top four and qualify for next season's Champions League.

articles.title: Kinsky set to return as Vicario awaits hernia surgery
articles.summary: Tottenham goalkeeper Guglielmo Vicario will have surgery on a hernia problem, paving the way for Antonin Kinsky to return to the side.

articles.title: Tailgating at matches to become criminal offence
articles.summary: Entering a football match in England and Wales without a ticket will become a criminal offence under new laws.

articles.title: Has Rosenior made his goalkeeping problem worse?
articles.summary: There is increased scrutiny of Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior, with his decision not to name a first-choice goalkeeper chief among the criticism he now faces.

articles.title: D-day for Livi in Kilmarnock; McInnes & Stewart in focus
articles.summary: There is a crucial game at the foot of the Scottish Premiership table, while Falkirk's in-form Barney Stewart and Heart of Midlothian manager Derek McInnes are also in the spotlight.

articles.title: Premier League quiz: The best teams to go down and worst teams to stay up
articles.summary: To celebrate an especially competitive relegation battle, test yourself on the best Premier League teams to go down and the worst to stay up.

articles.title: Vote: What is Scotland's most iconic goal?
articles.summary: Vote in the first round to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

articles.title: Football Daily
articles.summary: John Murray & Ian Dennis are with producer Gary Flintoff. Also hear from Thomas Tuchel.

articles.title: Scottish refs' chief seeks talks with clubs over 'DOGSO' red cards
articles.summary: Scottish head of refereeing Willie Collum wants to discuss red cards where a player has committed denial of a goalscoring opportunity with clubs.

articles.title: Rowett focused on Foxes safety, not cost of relegation
articles.summary: Leicester City manager Gary Rowett says he will not let the potential cost of relegation distract him from trying to guide the Foxes to safety.

articles.title: Im clings on to lead at Valspar Championship as Hovland misses cut
articles.summary: Sungjae Im held onto his lead at the Valspar Championship after rallying on the back nine to edge ahead of David Lipsky.

articles.title: Devine at the double as Preston beat Stoke
articles.summary: Alfie Devine scored twice as Preston ended a seven-game winless streak with a 3-1 Championship victory over Stoke at Deepdale.

articles.title: Preston take major step towards Championship survival after easing past Stoke
articles.summary: Highlights of the Sky Bet Championship match between Preston North End and Stoke City.

articles.title: St Helens cruise to Toulouse win as Bradford beat Huddersfield
articles.summary: St Helens extended their winning streak to four games as a 30-16 victory at Toulouse lift them up to second place in the Betfred Super League.

articles.title: Belief, youth and defence - will NI cause Italy upset?
articles.summary: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill sits down with BBC Sport Northern Ireland before the World Cup play-off in Italy.

articles.title: Would WSL meet Fifa's new female coach rules?
articles.summary: BBC Sport explores whether the Women's Super League would be ready for Fifa's new female coach requirements.

articles.title: Northern Ireland 'should dream' of World Cup - McArdle
articles.summary: Northern Ireland manager Michael McArdle said his side "should always dream and strive" of qualifying for the Women's World Cup in 2027.

articles.title: History made as Messi hits 900 goals
articles.summary: Lionel Messi's scores his 900th goal in Inter Miami's match against Nashville SC in the Concacaf Champions Cup. The 38-year-old joins Cristiano Ronaldo in reaching the milestone. Watch some of his gre...

articles.title: Israel FA fined for 'discrimination and racist abuse'
articles.summary: Fifa fines the Israeli Football Association 150,000 Swiss Francs (£141,700) and warns it about its future conduct after investigating an "alleged offence of discrimination" by the Palestine FA.
